This kernel appears to be a synthetic/contrived microbenchmark rather than something from a known benchmark suite or tutorial. The specific characteristics — the arbitrary prime-number strides (17, 257), the bitwise AND masking with 4095 (to wrap within a 4096-element buffer), the hardcoded loop of 8 iterations with a multiply-accumulate pattern using 1.1f — look like someone hand-crafted a small test kernel to explore memory access patterns, cache behavior, or similar microarchitectural effects. It doesn't match any well-known benchmark (e.g., Rodinia, Parboil, SHOC) or standard tutorial pattern I'm aware of.
